observed_evidence.own_probes: Built my own hand-trace probes (distinct from the builder's checker fixtures): (P1) the crafted attack id brick-protocol-<expected>-gate-passed-event-evil-building-started-event-2026-06-25T00-00-00-00-00 -> only the rightmost '-building-started-event-' yields a fullmatch timestamp tail; '-gate-passed-event-' leaves tail 'evil-building-started-event-...' which fails fullmatch -> single candidate -> returns 'brick-protocol-<expected>-gate-passed-event-evil' which is NOT in _allowed_report_source_ids -> REJECTED. (P2) Determinism: for two DISTINCT marker strings to tie on source_id length they would have to start at the same offset in one string, which is impossible; the legacy endswith branch likewise admits at most one suffix (no legacy suffix is a suffix of another) -> the PYTHONHASHSEED nondeterminism is removed in practice. (P3) False-admit reachability: a valid candidate equal to '<expected>'/'brick-protocol-<expected>' requires report_id == '<expected>-<suffix>-<valid-ts>' i.e. literally expected's own legit report; no foreign building emits it -> no false-admit path found. (P4) False-reject: legit forms parse to brick-protocol-<bid> because nothing marker-like follows the genuine trailing marker.

No live admit/reject was executed.
